naive_cost: Rerunning all 47 frontier nodes with multiple encodings would spend at least 2,820 solver-seconds per method before proof replay and would duplicate 15 durable closures.
opportunities_considered: Exclude all 15 globally transferred replay-verified closures.
Use sequential only because it earned every unique benchmark closure; allocate zero generic kmtotalizer work.
Prioritize the fifth-orbit structural discriminator, then smaller inherited CNFs, and defer predecessor-blocker timeout cases.
Checkpoint every eight leaves so proof growth, replay reliability, and closure yield can redirect later segments.
Reuse the audited five-orbit blocker and immutable frontier bindings rather than reconstructing the partition.
chosen_reductions: Run exactly the 32 audited open nodes with one 60-second cold sequential attempt each, in an evidence-based order. Preserve every exact CNF, proof, semantic audit, and replay receipt; review after each eight-node segment.
expected_throughput_gain: At least 32% fewer solver attempts than a 47-node sweep, plus elimination of the noncompetitive second encoding. Early ordering should expose closure yield and proof-storage cost before committing all four segments.
soundness_basis: The active blocker/frontier is independently audited, the old blocker is a strict subset, all 15 transferred UNSAT proofs replayed, and every new closure requires reconstructed-CNF audit plus independent DRAT replay before ledger ingestion.
remains_uncompressed: Each of the 32 open canonical leaves remains an independent soundness obligation. No node is inferred closed from runtime, structural similarity, a provisional UNSAT status, or another leaf's certificate.
